briefdescription.para: Convenience value when no options are specified.
LE Coded using S=2 coding preferred when transmitting.
LE Coded using S=8 coding preferred when transmitting.
AA-Only RTT variant is not supported.
Sounding Sequence RTT variant is not supported.
Random Payload RTT variant is not supported.
Main mode 3 (RTT and PBR), sub-mode: unused.

detaileddescription.para: Represents the combination of Channel Sounding (CS) main mode and sub-mode.

simplesect.para: The underlying numeric values are an internal encoding and are not stable API. Do not assume a direct concatenation of HCI values when inspecting the raw enum value.

briefdescription.para: Channel sounding PHY used for CS sync.
Use Channel Selection Algorithm #3b for non-mode-0 CS steps.
Use Channel Selection Algorithm #3c for non-mode-0 CS steps.
Use Hat shape for user-specified channel sequence.
Use X shape for user-specified channel sequence.
Channel connected and ready for upper layer traffic on it.
Level 0: Only for BR/EDR special cases, like SDP.
Level 1: No encryption and no authentication.
Level 2: Encryption and no authentication (no MITM).
Level 4: Authenticated Secure Connections and 128-bit key.
Bit to force new pairing procedure, bit-wise OR with requested security level.
Paired with Out of Band method.
Convenience macro for when no PHY is set.
LE Coded PHY using S=8 coding.
LE Coded PHY using S=2 coding.
Path Loss zone that has been entered.

detaileddescription.para: The path loss zone that has been entered in the most recent LE Path Loss Monitoring Threshold Change event as documented in Core Spec. Version 5.4 Vol.4, Part E, 7.7.65.32.

simplesect.para: BT_CONN_LE_PATH_LOSS_ZONE_UNAVAILABLE has been added to notify when path loss becomes unavailable.

detaileddescription.para: The numeric values are the same as in the Core specification for Pairing Keypress Notification PDU.

briefdescription.para: Convenience value when no options are specified.

para.verbatim: Enable scanning on the LE Coded PHY.

Disable scanning on the LE 1M PHY.
@note Requires @ref BT_CONN_LE_OPT_CODED.

briefdescription.para: PIN or encryption key is missing.
The requested security level could not be reached.
Pairing failed but the exact reason could not be specified.
CS Test Tone Antenna Config Selection.

detaileddescription.para: These enum values are indices in the following table, where N_AP is the maximum number of antenna paths (in the range [1, 4]).
There are therefore four groups of possible antenna configurations:

listitem.para: 1:1 configuration, where both A and B support 1 antenna each
1:N_AP configuration, where A supports 1 antenna, B supports N_AP antennas, and N_AP is a value in the range [2, 4]
N_AP:1 configuration, where A supports N_AP antennas, B supports 1 antenna, and N_AP is a value in the range [2, 4]
2:2 configuration, where both A and B support 2 antennas and N_AP = 4

detaileddescription.para: Increment the reference count of a connection object.

simplesect.para: Will return NULL if the reference count is zero.
Connection object with incremented reference count, or NULL if the reference count is zero.

detaileddescription.para: Decrement the reference count of a connection object.

memberdef.argsstring: (enum bt_conn_type type, void(*func)(struct bt_conn *conn, void *data), void *data)
memberdef.name: bt_conn_foreach

detaileddescription.para: Iterates through all bt_conn objects that are alive in the Host allocator.
To find established connections, combine this with bt_conn_get_info. Check that bt_conn_info::state is BT_CONN_STATE_CONNECTED.

Thread safety: This API is thread safe, but it does not guarantee a sequentially-consistent view for objects allocated during the current invocation of this API. E.g. If preempted while allocations A then B then C happen then results may include A and C but miss B.

parameterdescription.para: Function to call for each connection.
Data to pass to the callback function.

briefdescription.para: Look up an existing connection by address.

detaileddescription.para: Look up an existing connection based on the remote address.
The caller gets a new reference to the connection object which must be released with bt_conn_unref() once done using the object.

simplesect.para: Connection object or NULL if not found.

briefdescription.para: Get destination (peer) address of a connection.

simplesect.para: Destination address if conn is a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE connection

briefdescription.para: Get array index of a connection.

detaileddescription.para: This function is used to map bt_conn to index of an array of connections. The array has CONFIG_BT_MAX_CONN elements.

simplesect.para: Index of the connection object. The range of the returned value is 0..CONFIG_BT_MAX_CONN-1
Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Function to determine the type of a connection.

parameterdescription.para: The types to check against. It is possible to supply multiple types, e.g. BT_CONN_TYPE_LE | BT_CONN_TYPE_SCO.
conn is either NULL or not of type type

briefdescription.para: Get connection info for the remote device.

simplesect.para: In order to retrieve the remote version (version, manufacturer and subversion) CONFIG_BT_REMOTE_VERSION must be enabled

The remote information is exchanged directly after the connection has been established. The application can be notified about when the remote information is available through the remote_info_available callback.
Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.
-EBUSY The remote information is not yet available.
-EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE or BT_CONN_TYPE_BR connection.
Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.
-ENOBUFS HCI command buffer is not available.
-EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE connection.

briefdescription.para: Get local enhanced connection transmit power level.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

parameterdescription.para: HCI command buffer is not available.

simplesect.para: -EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE connection.
Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

parameterdescription.para: HCI command buffer is not available.

simplesect.para: -EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE connection.
Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

parameterdescription.para: HCI command buffer is not available.

simplesect.para: -EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE connection.

detaileddescription.para: Change the configuration for path loss threshold change events for a given conn handle.

simplesect.para: To use this API CONFIG_BT_PATH_LOSS_MONITORING must be set.
Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.
-EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE connection.

briefdescription.para: Enable or Disable Path Loss Monitoring.

detaileddescription.para: Enable or disable Path Loss Monitoring, which will decide whether Path Loss Threshold events are sent from the controller to the host.

simplesect.para: To use this API CONFIG_BT_PATH_LOSS_MONITORING must be set.
Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.
-EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE connection.

detaileddescription.para: Change the default subrating parameters for all future ACL connections where the local device is the central. This command does not affect any existing connection. Parameters set for specific connection will always have precedence.

simplesect.para: To use this API CONFIG_BT_SUBRATING and CONFIG_BT_CENTRAL must be set.
Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

detaileddescription.para: Request a change to the subrating parameters of a connection.

simplesect.para: To use this API CONFIG_BT_SUBRATING must be set.
Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.
-EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE connection.

briefdescription.para: Read Minimum Supported Connection Interval Groups.

detaileddescription.para: Read the minimum supported connection interval and supported interval groups from the local controller. This information describes what the local controller supports.

simplesect.para: bt_conn_le_read_min_conn_interval if only bt_conn_le_min_conn_interval_info::min_supported_conn_interval_us is needed.
Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_BT_SHORTER_CONNECTION_INTERVALS.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to structure to receive the minimum connection interval information.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

detaileddescription.para: Read the minimum supported connection interval from the local controller.

simplesect.para: bt_conn_le_read_min_conn_interval_groups if groups are needed.
Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_BT_SHORTER_CONNECTION_INTERVALS.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to variable to receive the minimum connection interval in microseconds.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

detaileddescription.para: Set default connection rate parameters to be used for future connections. This command does not affect any existing connection. Parameters set for specific connection will always have precedence.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_BT_SHORTER_CONNECTION_INTERVALS.
Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

detaileddescription.para: Request a change to the connection parameters of a connection. This includes Subrate parameters. This allows changing the connection interval to below the Baseline ConnInterval Values (BCV) and with finer granularity, if supported.
Valid intervals of the local and peer controller should be known. See bt_conn_le_read_min_conn_interval_groups

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_BT_SHORTER_CONNECTION_INTERVALS.
Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.
-EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE connection.

detaileddescription.para: Request remote feature pages, from 0 up to pages_requested or the number of pages supported by the peer. There is a maximum of 10 pages. This function will trigger the read_all_remote_feat_complete callback when the procedure is completed.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_BT_LE_EXTENDED_FEAT_SET.

parameterdescription.para: Number of feature pages to be requested from peer. There is a maximum of 10 pages.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.
-EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE connection.

detaileddescription.para: Request a change to the frame space parameters of a connection. This function will trigger the frame_space_updated callback when the procedure is completed.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_BT_FRAME_SPACE_UPDATE..
Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.
-EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE connection.

detaileddescription.para: If the local device is in the peripheral role then updating the connection parameters will be delayed. This delay can be configured by through the CONFIG_BT_CONN_PARAM_UPDATE_TIMEOUT option.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.
-EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE connection.

briefdescription.para: Update the connection transmit data length parameters.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.
-EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE connection.

detaileddescription.para: Update the preferred transmit and receive PHYs of the connection. Use BT_GAP_LE_PHY_NONE to indicate no preference.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.
-EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE connection.

briefdescription.para: Update the default PHY parameters to be used for all subsequent connections over the LE transport.

detaileddescription.para: Update the preferred transmit and receive PHYs of LE transport. Use BT_GAP_LE_PHY_NONE to indicate no preference. For possible PHY values see bt_gap_le_phy.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Disconnect from a remote device or cancel pending connection.

detaileddescription.para: Disconnect an active connection with the specified reason code or cancel pending outgoing connection.
The disconnect reason for a normal disconnect should be: BT_HCI_ERR_REMOTE_USER_TERM_CONN.

The following disconnect reasons are accepted:
BT_HCI_ERR_AUTH_FAIL
BT_HCI_ERR_REMOTE_USER_TERM_CONN
BT_HCI_ERR_REMOTE_LOW_RESOURCES
BT_HCI_ERR_REMOTE_POWER_OFF
BT_HCI_ERR_UNSUPP_REMOTE_FEATURE
BT_HCI_ERR_PAIRING_NOT_SUPPORTED
BT_HCI_ERR_UNACCEPT_CONN_PARAM

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const bt_addr_le_t *peer, const struct bt_conn_le_create_param *create_param, const struct bt_le_conn_param *conn_param, struct bt_conn **conn)
memberdef.name: bt_conn_le_create

briefdescription.para: Initiate an LE connection to a remote device.

detaileddescription.para: Allows initiate new LE link to remote peer using its address.

The caller gets a new reference to the connection object which must be released with bt_conn_unref() once done using the object. If CONFIG_BT_CONN_CHECK_NULL_BEFORE_CREATE is enabled, this function will return -EINVAL if dereferenced conn is not NULL.
This uses the General Connection Establishment procedure.
The application must disable explicit scanning before initiating a new LE connection if CONFIG_BT_SCAN_AND_INITIATE_IN_PARALLEL is not enabled.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct bt_le_ext_adv *adv, const struct bt_conn_le_create_synced_param *synced_param, const struct bt_le_conn_param *conn_param, struct bt_conn **conn)
memberdef.name: bt_conn_le_create_synced

briefdescription.para: Create a connection to a synced device.

detaileddescription.para: Initiate a connection to a synced device from a Periodic Advertising with Responses (PAwR) train.

The caller gets a new reference to the connection object which must be released with bt_conn_unref() once done using the object. If CONFIG_BT_CONN_CHECK_NULL_BEFORE_CREATE is enabled, this function will return -EINVAL if dereferenced conn is not NULL.
This uses the Periodic Advertising Connection Procedure.

parameterdescription.para: The adverting set the PAwR advertiser belongs to.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Automatically connect to remote devices in the filter accept list.

detaileddescription.para: This uses the Auto Connection Establishment procedure. The procedure will continue until a single connection is established or the procedure is stopped through bt_conn_create_auto_stop. To establish connections to all devices in the filter accept list the procedure should be started again in the connected callback after a new connection has been established.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.
-ENOMEM No free connection object available.
Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Set security level for a connection.

detaileddescription.para: This function enable security (encryption) for a connection. If the device has bond information for the peer with sufficiently strong key encryption will be enabled. If the connection is already encrypted with sufficiently strong key this function does nothing.

If the device has no bond information for the peer and is not already paired then the pairing procedure will be initiated. Note that sec has no effect on the security level selected for the pairing process. The selection is instead controlled by the values of the registered bt_conn_auth_cb. If the device has bond information or is already paired and the keys are too weak then the pairing procedure will be initiated.

This function may return an error if the required level of security defined using sec is not possible to achieve due to local or remote device limitation (e.g., input output capabilities), or if the maximum number of paired devices has been reached.
This function may return an error if the pairing procedure has already been initiated by the local device or the peer device.

simplesect.para: When CONFIG_BT_SMP_SC_ONLY is enabled then the security level will always be level 4.
When CONFIG_BT_SMP_OOB_LEGACY_PAIR_ONLY is enabled then the security level will always be level 3.
When BT_SECURITY_FORCE_PAIR within sec is enabled then the pairing procedure will always be initiated.
0 on success or negative error
-EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE or BT_CONN_TYPE_BR connection.

briefdescription.para: Get security level for a connection.

simplesect.para: Connection security level if CONFIG_BT_SMP or CONFIG_BT_CLASSIC is enabled, else BT_SECURITY_L1
BT_SECURITY_L0 conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE or BT_CONN_TYPE_BR connection.

detaileddescription.para: This function gets encryption key size. If there is no security (encryption) enabled 0 will be returned.

simplesect.para: Encryption key size.
0 conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE or BT_CONN_TYPE_BR connection.

detaileddescription.para: Register callbacks to monitor the state of connections.

parameterdescription.para: Callback struct. Must point to memory that remains valid.

detaileddescription.para: Unregister the state of connections callbacks.

parameterdescription.para: Callback struct point to memory that remains valid.

briefdescription.para: Converts a security error to string.

simplesect.para: The string representation of the security error code. If CONFIG_BT_SECURITY_ERR_TO_STR is not enabled, this just returns the empty string

detaileddescription.para: Set/clear the Bonding flag in the Authentication Requirements of SMP Pairing Request/Response data. The initial value of this flag depends on BT_BONDABLE Kconfig setting. For the vast majority of applications calling this function shouldn't be needed.
Get current bonding flag. The initial value of this flag depends on CONFIG_BT_BONDABLE Kconfig setting. The Bonding flag can be updated using bt_set_bondable().

simplesect.para: Current bonding flag.

briefdescription.para: Set/clear the bonding flag for a given connection.

detaileddescription.para: Set/clear the Bonding flag in the Authentication Requirements of SMP Pairing Request/Response data for a given connection.
The bonding flag for a given connection cannot be set/cleared if security procedures in the SMP module have already started. This function can be called only once per connection.

If the bonding flag is not set/cleared for a given connection, the value will depend on global configuration which is set using bt_set_bondable. The default value of the global configuration is defined using CONFIG_BT_BONDABLE Kconfig option.

simplesect.para: -EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE or BT_CONN_TYPE_BR connection
-EINVAL conn is a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE but could not get SMP context

briefdescription.para: Allow/disallow remote LE SC OOB data to be used for pairing.

detaileddescription.para: Set/clear the OOB data flag for LE SC SMP Pairing Request/Response data.

parameterdescription.para: Value allowing/disallowing remote LE SC OOB data.

briefdescription.para: Allow/disallow remote legacy OOB data to be used for pairing.

detaileddescription.para: Set/clear the OOB data flag for legacy SMP Pairing Request/Response data.

parameterdescription.para: Value allowing/disallowing remote legacy OOB data.

briefdescription.para: Set OOB Temporary Key to be used for pairing.

detaileddescription.para: This function allows to set OOB data for the LE legacy pairing procedure. The function should only be called in response to the oob_data_request() callback provided that the legacy method is user pairing.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to 16 byte long TK array. The TK value should be generated randomly for each new pairing process.

simplesect.para: -EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE connection.
-EINVAL tk is NULL.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_conn *conn, const struct bt_le_oob_sc_data *oobd_local, const struct bt_le_oob_sc_data *oobd_remote)
memberdef.name: bt_le_oob_set_sc_data

briefdescription.para: Set OOB data during LE Secure Connections (SC) pairing procedure.

detaileddescription.para: This function allows to set OOB data during the LE SC pairing procedure. The function should only be called in response to the oob_data_request() callback provided that LE SC method is used for pairing.

The user should submit OOB data according to the information received in the callback. This may yield three different configurations: with only local OOB data present, with only remote OOB data present or with both local and remote OOB data present.

parameterdescription.para: Local OOB data or NULL if not present
Remote OOB data or NULL if not present

simplesect.para: Zero on success or error code otherwise, positive in case of protocol error or negative (POSIX) in case of stack internal error.
-EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE connection.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_conn *conn, const struct bt_le_oob_sc_data **oobd_local, const struct bt_le_oob_sc_data **oobd_remote)
memberdef.name: bt_le_oob_get_sc_data

briefdescription.para: Get OOB data used for LE Secure Connections (SC) pairing procedure.

detaileddescription.para: This function allows to get OOB data during the LE SC pairing procedure that were set by the bt_le_oob_set_sc_data() API.

simplesect.para: The OOB data will only be available as long as the connection object associated with it is valid.

parameterdescription.para: Local OOB data or NULL if not set
Remote OOB data or NULL if not set

simplesect.para: Zero on success or error code otherwise, positive in case of protocol error or negative (POSIX) in case of stack internal error.
-EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE connection.

briefdescription.para: Set a fixed passkey to be used for pairing.

detaileddescription.para: This API is only available when the CONFIG_BT_FIXED_PASSKEY configuration option has been enabled.
Sets a fixed passkey to be used for pairing. If set, the pairing_confirm() callback will be called for all incoming pairings.

xrefdescription.para: Use BT_PASSKEY_RAND and the app_passkey callback from bt_conn_auth_cb instead.

parameterdescription.para: A valid passkey (0 - 999999) or BT_PASSKEY_INVALID to disable a previously set fixed passkey.

simplesect.para: 0 on success or a negative error code on failure.

detaileddescription.para: Register callbacks to handle authenticated pairing. Passing NULL unregisters a previous callbacks structure.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or negative error code otherwise

briefdescription.para: Overlay authentication callbacks used for a given connection.

detaileddescription.para: This function can be used only for Bluetooth LE connections. The CONFIG_BT_SMP must be enabled for this function.
The authentication callbacks for a given connection cannot be overlaid if security procedures in the SMP module have already started. This function can be called only once per connection.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or negative error code otherwise
-EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE or BT_CONN_TYPE_BR connection

detaileddescription.para: Register callbacks to get authenticated pairing information. Multiple registrations can be done.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or negative error code otherwise

detaileddescription.para: Unregister callbacks to stop getting authenticated pairing information.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or negative error code otherwise

detaileddescription.para: This function should be called only after passkey_entry callback from bt_conn_auth_cb structure was called.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or negative error code otherwise
-EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE or BT_CONN_TYPE_BR connection

briefdescription.para: Send Passkey Keypress Notification during pairing.

detaileddescription.para: This function may be called only after passkey_entry callback from bt_conn_auth_cb structure was called.
Requires CONFIG_BT_PASSKEY_KEYPRESS.

parameterdescription.para: BT_CONN_TYPE_LE destination connection for the notification.
What keypress event type to send.

simplesect.para: bt_conn_auth_keypress.
-EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE connection

detaileddescription.para: This function allows to cancel ongoing authenticated pairing.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or negative error code otherwise
-EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE or BT_CONN_TYPE_BR connection

briefdescription.para: Reply if passkey was confirmed to match by user.

detaileddescription.para: This function should be called only after passkey_confirm callback from bt_conn_auth_cb structure was called.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or negative error code otherwise
-EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE or BT_CONN_TYPE_BR connection

briefdescription.para: Reply if incoming pairing was confirmed by user.

detaileddescription.para: This function should be called only after pairing_confirm callback from bt_conn_auth_cb structure was called if user confirmed incoming pairing.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or negative error code otherwise
-EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_LE or BT_CONN_TYPE_BR connection

detaileddescription.para: This function should be called only after PIN code callback from bt_conn_auth_cb structure was called. It's for legacy 2.0 devices.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or negative error code otherwise
-EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_BR connection

briefdescription.para: Initiate an BR/EDR connection to a remote device.

detaileddescription.para: Allows initiate new BR/EDR link to remote peer using its address.
The caller gets a new reference to the connection object which must be released with bt_conn_unref() once done using the object.

simplesect.para: Valid connection object on success or NULL otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Look up an existing BR connection by address.

detaileddescription.para: Look up an existing BR connection based on the remote address.
The caller gets a new reference to the connection object which must be released with bt_conn_unref() once done using the object.

simplesect.para: Connection object or NULL if not found.

briefdescription.para: Get destination (peer) address of a connection.

simplesect.para: Destination address if conn is a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_BR connection

briefdescription.para: Change the role of the conn.

parameterdescription.para: The role that want to switch as, the value is BT_HCI_ROLE_CENTRAL and BT_HCI_ROLE_PERIPHERAL from hci_types.h.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.
-ENOBUFS HCI command buffer is not available.
-EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_BR connection

briefdescription.para: Enable/disable role switch of the connection by setting the connection's link policy.

parameterdescription.para: Value enable/disable role switch of controller.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.
-ENOBUFS HCI command buffer is not available.
-EINVAL conn is not a valid BT_CONN_TYPE_BR connection.

briefdescription.para: Helper to declare connection parameters inline.
Default LE connection parameters: Connection Interval: 30-50 ms Latency: 0 Timeout: 4 s.
Helper to declare PHY parameters inline.

memberdef.name: BT_CONN_LE_PHY_PARAM_ALL
memberdef.initializer: BT_CONN_LE_PHY_PARAM(BT_GAP_LE_PHY_1M | \ BT_GAP_LE_PHY_2M | \ BT_GAP_LE_PHY_CODED, \ BT_GAP_LE_PHY_1M | \ BT_GAP_LE_PHY_2M | \ BT_GAP_LE_PHY_CODED)

parameterdescription.para: Maximum Link Layer transmission payload size in bytes.
Maximum Link Layer transmission payload time in us.

briefdescription.para: Helper to declare transmit data length parameters inline.

parameterdescription.para: Maximum Link Layer transmission payload size in bytes.
Maximum Link Layer transmission payload time in us.

detaileddescription.para: The practical maximum is 41 groups based on HCI event size constraints: (HCI event max size - cmd_complete overhead - response fields) / sizeof(group) (255 - 4 - 3) / 6 = 41 groups max
As defined in Bluetooth Core Specification, Vol 4, Part E, Section 7.8.151.

briefdescription.para: Extract main mode part from bt_conn_le_cs_mode.

simplesect.para: Returned values match the HCI main mode values.

The value 0 encodes HCI 0xFF. This allows bt_conn_le_cs_mode to fit in one byte. To obtain the HCI sub-mode value, use (sub_mode == 0 ? 0xFF : sub_mode), where sub_mode is the value returned by this macro.

detaileddescription.para: Multiply by 1.25 to get milliseconds.
Note that this may be inaccurate, as something like 7.5 ms cannot be accurately presented with integers.
Multiply by 1250 to get microseconds.

briefdescription.para: Convert shorter connection interval to microseconds.

detaileddescription.para: Multiply by 125 to get microseconds.

briefdescription.para: Helper to declare create connection parameters inline.

detaileddescription.para: Scan continuously by setting scan interval equal to scan window.

briefdescription.para: Default LE create connection using filter accept list parameters.

detaileddescription.para: Scan window: 30 ms. Scan interval: 60 ms.

briefdescription.para: Register a callback structure for connection events.

detaileddescription.para: Special passkey value that can be used to disable a previously set fixed passkey.

briefdescription.para: Special passkey value that can be used to generate a random passkey when using the app_passkey callback from bt_conn_auth_cb.

parameterdescription.para: True if role switch is allowed

briefdescription.para: Helper to declare BR/EDR connection parameters inline.

parameterdescription.para: True if role switch is allowed

briefdescription.para: Default BR/EDR connection parameters: Role switch allowed.
